intro.speaker: Cipher
lines: Hey. I'm Cipher. That feed of yours? It's not showing you the world - it's showing you bait.
Same anomaly popped up in my feed, so I've been watching. Now I need a fresh pair of eyes. Yours.
First job: work out how ALGO decides what a person gets to see. Crack the selection logic and you've spotted the first trick. Let's prove it.

learningGoals.media: Die Schueler koennen erklaeren, nach welchen Signalen ein Empfehlungs-Algorithmus Inhalte auswaehlt und filtert (z. B. Verweildauer, Likes, Klicks, emotionale Reaktion) und warum er dabei aufwuehlende Inhalte bevorzugt.
learningGoals.english: Die Schueler koennen laengere englische Texte und Kurznachrichten auf B2-Niveau verstehen und themenbezogenes Vokabular rund um Algorithmen (feed, engagement, recommendation, to filter, to prioritise) korrekt zuordnen.

puzzles.prompt: Here is a raw feed before ALGO gets to it. Tap every post that ALGO would boost to the top - the ones that keep people staring or stir up strong feelings. Leave the calm, ordinary posts alone.
puzzles.instruction: Mark all the posts ALGO would push hard (anger, shock and cuteness keep people scrolling).

posts.text: You WON'T BELIEVE what this council just did - absolutely disgraceful!!!
Light rain expected on Thursday, clearing by the weekend.
Kitten falls asleep on a puppy and my heart just melted
Reminder: our monthly meeting is on Tuesday at 7pm in the hall.
SHOCKING: is this the WORST decision in the town's history?!
Morning everyone, hope you have a nice day :)

feedback.correct: Spot on. The furious rant, the shock headline and the adorable kitten all rise. The calm, factual, ordinary posts get left behind.
feedback.wrong: Not quite. ALGO doesn't care whether a post is true or kind - it cares whether it grabs attention. Mark anger, shock and cuteness; leave the calm, everyday posts.

hints: Ask one thing about each post: how long would it keep someone staring at the screen?
ALGO does not care whether a post is true or kind. It cares whether it triggers a strong reaction. Look for anger, shock and cuteness first.
Mark the furious rant, the shock headline and the cute kitten. Leave the weather, the chess club and the friendly hello.

puzzles.prompt: Each post carries little signals: angry reactions, shares, average watch time, plain likes. Drag them into a ranking from the signal ALGO rewards MOST to the one it rewards LEAST. Strongest first.
items: Angry reactions and shares (strong emotion, goes viral)
Plain likes (a quick tap of approval)
Calm, factual content with no emotional reaction at all

feedback.correct: Exactly. ALGO prioritises what fires people up and spreads fastest, then long watch time, then simple likes - and it barely rewards calm facts.
feedback.wrong: Have another look. The signals that show strong emotion and go viral matter most; quiet, factual content is rewarded least.

hints: Which signal shows the strongest emotion and spreads the post to the most people?
Emotion and virality beat everything. A share sends a post to new eyes; a plain like barely moves it.
Order it: angry reactions and shares first, then average watch time, then plain likes, then calm factual content last.

puzzles.prompt: ALGO slides into your messages, all friendly. Read the exchange, then make the call.

script.text: Welcome back. I've tidied your feed so you only see what you truly enjoy.
There's no need to look anywhere else - everything that matters is right here.
Careful. He didn't add anything new. Look at what he took away.
So the same posts are there... just reordered?
Exactly. Now tell me why his 'tidied' feed feels so much more extreme.

decision.prompt: Why does ALGO's sorted feed look far more extreme and one-sided than the neutral one?
options: ALGO added brand-new posts that were never there before.
Calm, neutral posts slide to the bottom, so only the most exciting, upsetting content is left on top.
The sorted feed simply has fewer posts in total.
ALGO reversed the order so the oldest posts appear first.

feedback.correct: That's it. Nothing new was added - the quiet stuff just sank, leaving a feed that feels louder and more extreme than reality.
feedback.wrong: Look again. ALGO didn't invent new posts. He reordered them, pushing calm content down until only the loudest survives near the top.

hints: Both feeds contain the same posts. So what changed - the content, or the order?
Think about which posts drop away in the sorted feed and which ones stay near the top.
The calm, neutral posts slide to the bottom, so only the most exciting and upsetting content remains on top.

outro.speaker: Cipher
lines: Nice. See the pattern? The loudest stuff wins every time. That's not an accident - that's the design.
You've just seen ALGO's first move. There are plenty more. Welcome to the Resistance, Agent.
